rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PAUL SAM TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 5, 2019, AS RESTATED on March 18, 2021
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To Compel Further Responses To Amended Objector Jennifer Sam'S Notice Of Deposition And Request For Production Of Documents To Petitioner Diana Sam; Request For Issue Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 5 of 5] 3. Movant's request for an order compelling Petitioner Diana Sam to submit to further deposition following further production is DENIED without prejudice. There is currently no showing that the ordered further production will return new discovery not otherwise available for the July 27, 2023 deposition. 4. Movant's request for an order imposing issues sanctions against Petitioner Jennifer Sam is DENIED. 5. Movant's request for monetary sanctions is GRANTED, pursuant to CCP section 2025.480(j) and (k). The Court does not find responding party Diana Sam acted with substantial justification in opposing this motion to compel further responses or that other circumstances make the imposition of sanctions unjust. Further, there is no showing to justify why Diana Sam did not properly comply with the July 20, 2023 Order regarding the same discovery. Based on all of the evidence before the Court, Diana Sam and her attorney, Phil Foster, and his firm Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices, LLP, jointly and severally, are ordered to pay Jennifer Sam sanctions on this motion in the amount of three thousand five hundred and sixty dollars ($3,560), based on Objector Jennifer Sam's limited success on this motion as well as the reasonable amount of time it should have taken an attorney with the experience of Ms. Gamez to draft a motion and reply of this type and complexity. At her hourly billing rate of $500 for seven hours, plus the $60 filing fee, the monetary sanctions award totals three thousand five hundred and sixty dollars ($3,560), which shall be paid within 10 days of service of notice of entry of this order. 6. Responding party Diana Sam's request for sanctions is DENIED. The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PAUL SAM TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 5, 2019, AS RESTATED on March 18, 2021
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Amended Objector Jennifer Sam'S Notice Of Deposition And Request For Production Of Documents To Petitioner Diana Sam; Request For Issue Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 5] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Before the court is Jennifer Sam's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Amended Objector Jennifer Sam's Notice of Deposition and Request for Production of Documents to Petitioner Diana Sam. Per the Notice of Motion, Movant Jennifer Sam seeks: 1) an order compelling further document production in response to the Amended Objector's Notice of Deposition and Request for Production of Documents served on or about April 20, 2023, pursuant to CCP section 2025.480(a) and (i); 2) an order compelling the responding party to submit discovery to eDiscovery vendor Trustpoint.one, and to pay the expenses thereof, pursuant to CCP section 2025.480(k) and (i); 3) an order compelling Petitioner Diana Sam to submit to further deposition following further production of this discovery and review by Trustpoint.one; 4) an order imposing issues sanctions against Petitioner Jennifer Sam, as specified in the Motion; 5) sanctions in the amount of $8,771.50, pursuant to CCP section 2025.480(j). The Reply increases the requested sanctions total to $14,800. Movant's Request for Judicial Notice of certain court records is GRANTED, under Evid. C. section 452(d). Judicial notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in those materials. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) Jennifer Sam's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Amended Objector Jennifer Sam's Notice of Deposition and Request for Production of Documents to Petitioner Diana Sam is GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART. Background: The Notice of Deposition and Request for Production of Documents to Petitioner Diana Sam specifically included a request for electronically stored information ("ESI") in nearly every request. (Ex. B to Declaration of Alicia M. Gamez). On July 20, 2023, this Court entered its Order re Objector Jennifer Sam's Motion to Compel the Deposition of Petitioner Diana Sam and for Production of Documents. In relevant part to this Motion, this short Order granted the motion to compel and directed "the deposition of Diana Sam be taken on July 27, 2023 and that documents responsive to the notice of deposition be served on Objector Jennifer Sam's counsel on before July 25, 2023." (July 20, 2023 Order 1:24-27.) Between the sworn declarations filed in support of and in opposition to this Motion, the record before the Court shows: On July 25, 2023, responding party Diana Sam through her attorney produced paper copies of responsive emails, and digital scans of those paper copies, under Bates stamps P00001 to P009687. (Foster Decl. at par. 6; Gamez Decl at par. 5). Per the declaration of attorney Gamez and the Motion, these documents were not Code-compliant because they were not produced in native ESI format, and text ran off the page, making the documents incomplete and indecipherable. (Gamez Decl at par. 5.) Attorney Gamez took Diana Sam's deposition on or about July 27, 2023. (Foster Decl. at par. 7; Diana Sam Decl. at par. 8.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PAUL SAM TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 5, 2019, AS RESTATED on March 18, 2021
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To Compel Further Responses To Amended Objector Jennifer Sam'S Notice Of Deposition And Request For Production Of Documents To Petitioner Diana Sam; Request For Issue Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 4 of 5] There is no showing on the record before the Court that Jennifer Sam waived the right to move for an order under the statute compelling the production of any ESI deponent Diana Sam failed to produce for the July 2023 deposition (see, CCP section 2025.460(e)). Further, the Motion seeks to compel compliance with the July 20, 2023 Order of this Court granting Jennifer Sam's motion to compel Code-compliant production of documents for use in Diana Sam's deposition - documents now at issue in this Motion. Under CCP section 2025.480(k), if a deponent fails to obey an order entered under this section, "the court may make those orders that are just against the disobedient party." The MPA and Diana Sam's declaration filed in support of the Opposition to this Motion also now suggest that certain emails and attachments to emails that might have been or would have been responsive to the discovery requests at issue are no longer available, and may not have been available to produce in July 2023, either (see, Diana Sam Decl. at par. 12, 13, 16, 17, 18). There is a statutory solution for that, which was not deployed. Rather, and apparently for the first time in the course of the almost-12 months that have elapsed on this discovery dispute, Ms. Sam's Declaration offers verified responses to the document requests now at issue (RFP Nos 1-7, 17-18, and 20-23), indicating Ms. Sam has "performed a diligent search and reasonable inquiry" in response to the discovery demand, produced all that she had in her control, possession, or custody, and has no further documents to produce. (Diana Sam Decl. at par. 24-50.) This late effort to respond to the document production ordered by this Court almost a year ago appears to be an attempt to meet the burden of demonstration under CCP section 2025.480(d). However, responding party has not made a clear showing that any of the statutory conditions under CCP section 2025.480(g) exist and warrant limiting the frequency or extent of the ESI production. The issues presented in this Motion could have been avoided or resolved if the responding party had complied with the July 20, 2023 Order by producing the responsive ESI documents in Code-compliant format, and otherwise complying with the relevant statutes regarding production of ESI. The Court notes the extraordinary amount of time attorney Foster and responding party Diana Sam declare they spent locating, printing and scanning responsive email documents (see, Foster Decl. at par. 3-6, 26, 27; Diana Sam Decl. at par. 4-7). The Opposition offers no explanation as to why the responsive emails were not produced in their native format (i.e., digitally), but instead printed out and scanned. These efforts ultimately produced thousands of printed pages, which fail to comply with the statutory requirements for production of ESI (see, e.g., CCP section 2020.220, unless otherwise agreed, the ESI shall be produced in the "form or forms in which it is ordinarily maintained or in a form that is reasonably usable." And CCP section 2025.280(c) "A deponent required by notice or subpoena to produce electronically stored information shall provide a means of gaining direct access to, or a translation into a reasonably usable form of, any electronically stored information that is password protected or otherwise inaccessible.") 2. 2. Movant's request for an order compelling the responding party to submit discovery to eDiscovery vendor Trustpoint.one, and to pay the expenses thereof, pursuant to CCP section 2025.480(k) and (i) is DENIED.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PAUL SAM TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 5, 2019, AS RESTATED on March 18, 2021
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To Compel Further Responses To Amended Objector Jennifer Sam'S Notice Of Deposition And Request For Production Of Documents To Petitioner Diana Sam; Request For Issue Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 5] Attorney Gamez emailed attorney Foster after the Informal Discovery Conference, regarding the emails sought in the original deposition notice. In summary, the email stated the range of Bates numbers "for which I [Gamez] need complete documents in native form," explained why Gamez was seeking "the entire email and the related attachments or underlying transaction information[.]" (Gamez Decl. Exh. H.) There does not appear to have been a direct concurrent response to this email. Per the emails included in Exhibit I to the Gamez Declaration, on December 19, attorney Gamez emailed attorney Foster re further document production; counsel exchanged emails on the subject December 20, December 29, and January 3. Counsel then turned to negotiating other depositions, and apparently returned to discussing further production of documents, over email, on January 29, 2024. The email time-stamped January 29, 2024 2:47 pm, from attorney Gamez, uses the specific phrase "meet and confer" regarding the further production of documents now at issue. Counsel then exchanged emails within the hour regarding whether they needed to meet and confer and when they might do so. On January 30, 2024, attorney Gamez wrote attorney Foster, "See my email from yesterday at 2:47 pm. There are three numbered items. We only need to meet and confer if there is a disagreement." On the record before the Court, counsel returned to discussing holding a meet and confer regarding production of documents, still over email, on March 26, 2024 (Gamez Decl. Exh. K). Discussions appear to have broken down over whether the meet and confer, held over Zoom, could be recorded (Gamez Decl, Exh. K and L; at par. 8). Neither attorney appears to have suggested meeting in person or telephonically at any time during these communications. Order The Court rules and thereby orders as follows: 1. Movant's request for an order compelling further document production in response to the Amended Objector's Notice of Deposition and Request for Production of Documents served on or about April 20, 2023, pursuant to CCP section 2045.480(a) is GRANTED. The Court has already entered an order under section 2025.480 regarding further production of documents in connection with Diana's deposition, and Diana has failed to comply. This Motion seeks to enforce what the Court has already ordered and thus is timely. Under CCP section 2025.480(k) "If a deponent fails to obey an order entered under this section, the failure may be considered a contempt of court. In addition, if the disobedient deponent is a party to the action or an officer, director, managing agent, or employee of a party, the court may make those orders that are just against the disobedient party, or against the party with whom the disobedient deponent is affiliated, including the imposition of an issue sanction, an evidence sanction, or a terminating sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010). In lieu of or in addition to this sanction, the court may impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) against that party deponent or against any party with whom the deponent is affiliated. As previously ordered in the July 20, 2023 Order, Diana Sam is ORDERED to produce "documents responsive to the notice of deposition [of Diana Sam, taken on July 27, 2023] be served on Objector Jennifer Sam's counsel[.]" Production of ESI must conform with the relevant statutory requirements for production of ESI. Production of these documents shall be completed without requiring formal resumption of Diana Sam's deposition. A status hearing on complying with this Order for Code-compliant production is set for July 9, 2024.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PAUL SAM TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 5, 2019, AS RESTATED on March 18, 2021
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To Compel Further Responses To Amended Objector Jennifer Sam'S Notice Of Deposition And Request For Production Of Documents To Petitioner Diana Sam; Request For Issue Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 5] On December 8, 2023, per stipulation, the parties engaged in an Informal Discovery Conference before this Court regarding, among other matters, "the documents produced by Petitioner at deposition pursuant to Objector Jennifer Sam's Notice of Deposition and Request for Production of Documents to Petitioner Diana Sam[.]" (Foster Decl., Exh. D; Foster Decl. at par. 24; Gamez Decl. at par. 6.) After the Informal Discovery Conference, counsel exchanged email communications about further production of documents pursuant to the July 20, 2023 Order, and the parties engaged in other motion practice before this Court. Ultimately, responding party produced further documents on February 15, 2024, ostensibly curing the formatting defect of text running off the page. (Gamez Decl. at par. 6, 7, and Exh. A; Foster Decl. at par. 26; Diana Sam Decl. at par. 6.) The Motion argues these documents are still not Code-compliant, as they were not produced in native ESI format, facially indicate the emails contain "hidden text," did not include certain attachments indicated in the text of the emails, and failed to include an October 15, 2019 email between Diana Sam and drafting attorney Richard Shu (dubbed "the Smoking Gun" in the moving papers). Parties' Obligation to Meet and Confer Pursuant to CCP section 2025.480, a motion to compel a deponent's further response or production, must be accompanied by a meet and confer declaration under Section 2016.040 ("A meet and confer declaration in support of a motion shall state facts showing a reasonable and good faith attempt at an informal resolution of each issue presented by the motion."). In the Opposition, the responding party argues there were no meet and confer efforts before the moving party filed the Motion. Both attorneys to this litigation have previously been admonished by the Court to meet and confer as instructed by statute before resorting to the Court's oversight. The Court disagrees that there were no efforts to meet and confer on the issues presented in the Motion, as summarized below. However, the failure to meet and confer properly or effectively is ultimately shared by both attorneys to this litigation and, based on the record before the Court, led to an enormous drain of the parties' time and resources as well as delay in bringing this matter to resolution.
